title: Plains & Midwest Severe Weather Outbreak — May 16-20, 2026
description: A three-day severe weather outbreak swept the central Plains and Upper Midwest from May 16-18, 2026, producing at least 17 confirmed tornadoes across Illinois, Nebraska, Kansas, and Iowa. The SPC issued Enhanced Risk on May 17 and upgraded to Moderate Risk on May 18, with strong tornado potential (EF2+) and very large hail across Kansas, Nebraska, Iowa, and northwest Missouri. Track post-event storm reports, damage assessments, and continued weather impacts.
impacts: Supercell thunderstorms with hail up to 2-3 inches in diameter from Nebraska into western Iowa
Strong tornadoes possible Sunday afternoon and evening across the Enhanced Risk area
Damaging wind gusts of 60-80 mph as storms cluster and grow upscale Sunday night into southeast SD, northwest IA, and southwest MN
Excessive rainfall and flash flooding risk across northern Missouri, Iowa, and the Kansas/Nebraska border
Severe weather threat continues Monday across the Upper Midwest with damaging wind the primary hazard

seo.title: Plains & Midwest Severe Weather Tracker | May 16-18 2026
seo.description: Track the live May 2026 severe weather outbreak — a Moderate Risk for strong tornadoes and giant hail across Kansas, Nebraska, and Iowa on May 18, following two days of severe storms across the central Plains and Upper Midwest. Real-time alerts, radar, and storm reports.
seo.keywords: plains severe weather, midwest tornado outbreak, nebraska tornadoes, iowa severe weather, may 2026 severe weather, enhanced risk tornadoes
